2606.09674.title: (Auto)formalization is supposed to be easy: Trellis process semantics for spelling out rigorous proofs
2606.09674.paper_summary: We present Trellis: an autoformalization system that leverages LLM agents in a deterministically constrained workflow to enforce incremental progress in Lean autoformalization tasks through iterative refinement of natural language proofs. Our approach is motivated by the common mathematician's notion of what it means to have a rigorous proof in the first place: namely, that it would be routine to elaborate any part of the proof in further detail. The result is a system which aims to achieve reliable autoformalization on a modest budget and with generalist agents, with specialization to autoformalization coming not from any task-specific agent training but instead from a meaning-of-rigor inspired workflow enforced by process semantics. We link to an end-to-end Lean formalization of a recent Ramsey theory breakthrough produced by the process.
2606.09674.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了 Trellis：一個自動形式化系統，利用 LLM 代理在確定性約束的工作流程中強制在 Lean 自動形式化任務中通過自然語言證明的迭代精煉來實現增量進展。我們的方法受到普通數學家對於嚴謹證明的定義的啟發：即，詳細闡述證明的任何部分應該是常規的。其結果是一個旨在以適度預算和通才代理實現可靠自動形式化的系統，自動形式化的專業化不是來自任何特定任務的代理訓練，而是來自於一個受到嚴謹意義啟發的工作流程，並由過程語義強制執行。我們鏈接到由該過程產生的最近 Ramsey 理論突破的端到端 Lean 形式化。
2606.09674.author: Wesley Pegden et.al.
